title: Intimidator (Executor-class)
content:

{{Quote|Such a splendid vessel. The sight of it still inspires my blood.|[[Tal Fraan]]|<ref name="TT" />}}
The '''''Intimidator''''' was an [[Executor-class Star Dreadnought/Legends|''Executor''-class Star Dreadnought]] used by the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy's]] [[Sixth Negative Regions|Black Sword Command]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. Originally undergoing work at a [[Shipyard/Legends|shipyard]] in the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]], it was transferred to the Empire's [[Black Fifteen|Black-15]] [[Type II Orbital Repair Yard|shipyard]] above the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[N'zoth]] in the [[Koornacht Cluster/Legends|Koornacht Cluster]] to free up space for similarly-sized [[Warship/Legends|warships]]. Eight months after the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance's]] victory at the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], the [[Black Fleet]]—comprising the ''Intimidator'' and all warships present, along with their crews—was seized by N'zoth's native [[Yevetha]] workers under resistance leader [[Nil Spaar]] as part of their [[Yevethan Uprising|mutiny]] against the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperials]], who had been in the process of withdrawing all [[Imperial Military/Legends|military]] personnel and equipment from the Cluster.

Renamed the '''''Pride of Yevetha''''', the [[Super Star Destroyer/Legends|Super Star Destroyer]] served as Spaar's [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]] as he was elevated to [[Viceroy/Legends|Viceroy]] of the [[Duskhan League]] during the [[Black Fleet Crisis]], which pitted the Yevethan government against the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] under [[Chief of State (New Republic)/Legends|Chief of State]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa Solo]]. In [[16 ABY/Legends|16 ABY]], the ''Pride of Yevetha'' was one of numerous vessels that participated in the [[Yevethan Purge|Great Purge]], an ethnic cleansing of the Koornacht Cluster enacted by the [[Speciesism|xenophobic]] Yevetha. In an effort to determine the strength of the Duskhan League's forces, several [[21st Recon Group|New Republic reconnaissance flights]] were sent into the Cluster, leading to the discovery of the ''Pride of Yevetha'' in the [[N'zoth system/Legends|N'zoth system]]. The New Republic's leadership was shocked to learn that the Yevetha were in possession of a [[Star Dreadnought/Legends|Star Dreadnought]].

Unbeknownst to the Yevetha, [[Camp Pa'aal Squadron|Imperial personnel captured]] during the Yevethan mutiny thirteen years before had spent their [[Prison/Legends|imprisonment]] installing [[slave circuit]]ry into the Imperial-designed ships, including the Super Star Destroyer, in preparation for a revolt. Their work was completed by [[17 ABY/Legends|17 ABY]], just as a combined New Republic [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] arrived in the N'zoth [[Star system/Legends|system]] with an ultimatum for Spaar to surrender. Before the two fleets could begin the [[Battle of N'zoth]], Imperial leader [[Major/Legends|Major]] [[Sil Sorannan]] assumed command of the ''Intimidator''—at the same time rejecting its Yevethan name—and activated the slave circuitry, bringing all the Imperial warships of the Black Fleet into [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]], with the Imperial throne world of [[Byss/Legends|Byss]], in the [[Deep Core/Legends|Deep Core]], as their destination. However, unknown to the liberated Imperials, Byss had been destroyed [[11 ABY/Legends|six years earlier]]. The Black Fleet arrived at the [[Asteroid field/Legends|remains]] of the planet and, after a month, had gradually dispersed. The ''Intimidator'' disappeared, although it was discovered [[21 ABY/Legends|four years later]] near the [[Unknown Regions/Legends|Unknown Regions]], abandoned and destroyed.

==Description==
{{Quote|Now, that, that can only be the ''Intimidator''. All of the late-production Super-class had that additional shield tower located on the centerline—|Ayddar Nylykerka|<ref name="SOL" />}}
The ''Intimidator'' was a 19,000-[[Meter/Legends|meter]]-long<ref name="Length" /> [[Executor-class Star Dreadnought/Legends|''Executor''-class Star Dreadnought]],<ref name="Class" /> though like its sister [[Starship/Legends|ships]], the [[Warship/Legends|warship]] was sometimes referred to as a [[Super-class Star Destroyer|''Super''-class Star Destroyer]].<ref name="CSWE" /> The ''Intimidator'' was modified beyond its stock statistics; among its improvements was an additional [[Deflector shield generator/Legends|shield generator]] mounted on the ship's midline.<ref name="SOL" /> During the vessel's time in the hands of the [[Yevetha]], the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] captives of [[Camp Pa'aal Squadron]] installed [[slave circuit]]ry—a feature that enabled one person to assume remote command of an entire [[Fleet/Legends|fleet's]] worth of enslaved ships—into the ''Intimidator'' and the other ships of the [[Black Fleet]].<ref name="TT" /> The installation of the slave circuitry had been done piecemeal during their years of captivity,<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> and the last necessary components were assembled and delivered to their respective ships in [[17 ABY/Legends|17 ABY]] in preparation for an Imperial revolt.<ref name="TT" />

The Yevetha also made several modifications to the ''Intimidator''. ''[[Darama]]'' [[Nil Spaar]] had three of the warship's [[Prison/Legends|detention blocks]] transformed into [[Breedery|breederies]] for his offspring, each possessing forty-eight alcoves serving as [[Mara-nas|birth-casks]]. For the purpose of housing [[Prisoner/Legends|prisoners]], three of the Super [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer's]] cargo bays were converted into large holding cells, with minimal facilities capable of supporting a thousand beings. The breederies and holding cells were completed by [[16 ABY/Legends|16 ABY]].<ref name="TT" />

==History==
===Docked at the Black-15 shipyard===
{{Quote|Tell the crew that they are the prisoners of the Yevetha Protectorate and His Glory the viceroy. Tell them that beginning today, their lives depend on their being useful to us.|Nil Spaar|<ref name="BTS" />}}
The ''Intimidator'' was one of three [[Super Star Destroyer]]s assigned to the Black Fleet of [[Sixth Negative Regions|Black Sword Command]], a unit within the [[Imperial Military/Legends|Imperial Military]] tasked with the defense of the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]]. Eight months after the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance's]] victory and the [[Death/Legends|death]] of [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] at the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], the ''Intimidator'' was stationed at the [[Black Fifteen|Black-15]] [[Type II Orbital Repair Yard|shipyard]] above the Yevethan [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of [[N'zoth]] in the [[Koornacht Cluster/Legends|Koornacht Cluster]], having been transferred from the Core Worlds, where it had been undergoing work, to free up the necessary amount of space for construction of another ''Executor''-class vessel. Out of the nine ships undergoing repairs at the Black-15 dock, only the ''Intimidator'' was [[Realspace/Legends|spaceworthy]].<ref name="BTS" />

When Imperial [[Garrison/Legends|garrison]] [[Commander/Legends|commander]] [[Jian Paret]] received an order to withdraw all military personnel from the [[N'zoth system/Legends|system]] and destroy whatever had to be left behind, Paret used the ''Intimidator'' as his [[Command ship/Legends|command ship]] during the evacuation, having moved to it within an [[Standard hour/Legends|hour]] after receiving his orders. With a skeleton crew of only one-third of its capacity aboard, and the need to transfer all of the garrison's assets and personnel to the warship before destroying the repair yard, Paret was unable to leave as quickly as he had wanted. To avoid any potential hostile moves from the predominantly Yevetha work force, Paret hid the true nature of the Imperial evacuation behind the facade of a departure drill.<ref name="BTS" />

However, Yevetha underground leader Nil Spaar knew of the imminent Imperial withdrawal and had chosen the time to [[Yevethan Uprising|seize]] the Black-15 [[Shipyard/Legends|shipyard]] and all of the vessels present as part of a plan to revolt against the Imperials five years in the making. With the transfer of the [[Victory I-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Victory''-class Star Destroyer]] [[Harridan (starship)|''Harridan'']] from patrol duty to active service at the battlefield at [[Notak]], Spaar saw that the Imperials were essentially defenseless. In Sections 17 and 21 of the ''Intimidator'', over a dozen external hatches were rigged to report their status as secured and sealed during an emergency battle-stations lockdown. The Yevetha leader had taken additional precautions should the ship attempt to escape—a package of explosives, disarmed only by a constant signal, would destroy the ship if the [[Deflector shield/Legends|shields]] were activated, blocking the signal and detonating the bombs. With these measures in place, two Yevetha [[Commando/Legends|commando]] teams—one led by Spaar and the other by his second-in-command, [[Dar Bille]]—boarded the ''Intimidator'' through the rigged hatches. Silently and quickly, the two teams converged on the [[Bridge (starship)/Legends|bridge]] without having to fire a single shot. When they breached the command deck, they killed seven of the Imperial crew present before securing the remainder.<ref name="BTS" />

As the events unfolded, Imperial [[Governor/Legends|Governor]] [[Crollick]]'s [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttle]] and a convoy of Imperial [[Transport/Legends|transports]] rose from N'zoth's surface toward the ''Intimidator'' as the warship slid out of her berth at the shipyard. When the transports began decelerating in preparation for docking, Spaar ordered all of the warship's autotargeting batteries to open fire on the inbound ships, a move that killed 20,000 Imperials: [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]], [[Technician/Legends|technicians]], bureaucrats, and families. Spaar concluded the [[Blood/Legends|bloodshed]] by executing Paret with his [[dew claw]], a retractable blade that emerged from a Yevetha's wrist. The Yevetha leader wasted no time in announcing that the remaining crew were now the prisoners of the [[Duskhan League]], the Yevethan government.<ref name="BTS" /> The other eight Star Destroyers present at the shipyard were also captured.<ref name="CCW">''[[Coruscant and the Core Worlds]]''</ref> The captive Imperials, approximately 300,000 crew members from the entire fleet, were transferred to [[Pa'aal]], the primary [[Moon/Legends|moon]] of N'zoth, to serve the Yevetha in operating and constructing their warships.<ref name="TT" />

===In the service of the Yevetha===
{{Quote|Is it not invigorating, to see how much power has passed into the hands of the Blessed? Can any doubt that we are the children of the All, the inheritors of the ancient glory? How far shall that glory carry us, my young disciple? How much shall we claim with our ambition?|Nil Spaar, to Tal Fraan|<ref name="TT" />}}
[[File:YevethanPurge.jpg|thumb|left|200px|The Yevethan Purge, in which the ''Pride of Yevetha'' participated]]
By 16 ABY—twelve years after the failed Imperial evacuation of N'zoth—the Yevetha had learned how to operate the captured ships from their onetime oppressors, who were now prisoners themselves.<ref name="TEC" /> However, unbeknownst to their overseers, the Imperials had begun the installation of slave circuitry in all the former Imperial warships, work done piecemeal over the years in preparation for a counter-revolt.<ref name="TT" /> The ''Intimidator'' was renamed ''Pride of Yevetha'' and was pressed into service as the [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]] of ''Darama'' Nil Spaar, [[Viceroy/Legends|viceroy]] of the Duskhan League. At the time, Dar Bille served as the [[Primate (Yevethan rank)|primate]] of the warship. Spaar's personal staff—including his [[Darna|primary]] and secondary mates; his first attaché [[Eri Palle]]; and [[Vor Duull]], the [[Proctor (Yevethan)|proctor]] of information science from the [[Aramadia-class thrustship|thrustship]] [[Aramadia (Aramadia-class)|''Aramadia'']]—were transferred to the ''Pride of Yevetha'' as well. It was from the bridge of the [[Super Star Destroyer/Legends|Super Star Destroyer]] that Bille, acting on an edict from Spaar, ordered the Yevethan warships to begin the [[Yevethan Purge|Great Purge]], an ethnic cleansing of the Koornacht Cluster. The ''Pride of Yevetha'' participated in the extermination of a colony of [[Kubaz/Legends|Kubaz]] at [[Doornik-319|Morning's Bell]] and the capture of a former Imperial factory [[Farm/Legends|farm]] at [[Pirol-5]], while the rest of the Black Fleet succeeded in ridding the Cluster of the foreign "vermin."<ref name="BTS" />

Though [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[Chief of State (New Republic)/Legends|Chief of State]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa Solo]] was appalled by the Duskhan League's actions, the New Republic could not defend the conquered [[Planet/Legends|worlds]] of the Koornacht Cluster, as they were not members of the [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] government. Certain members of the [[New Republic Senate/Legends|New Republic Senate]] were directly opposed to Republic involvement in the conflict, but Organa Solo nonetheless issued an ultimatum for Spaar—the Duskhan League was to withdraw from the worlds it had seized, or the New Republic would declare [[Black Fleet Crisis|war]]. Spaar would not back down, however; the New Republic was unaware of the League's full military strength and the existence of the ''Intimidator'', which Spaar could use to his advantage.<ref name="BTS" /> The New Republic made the first move with a [[Battle of Doornik-319|blockade of Doornik-319]] that proved disastrous and prompted members of the [[Senate]] to move against their Chief of State.<ref name="SOL" />

While this internal strife continued, several New Republic [[Scout/Legends|reconnaissance]] flights were made into the Koornacht Cluster to ascertain the Duskhan League's military strength. [[Lieutenant/Legends|Lieutenant]] [[Rone Taggar]] of the [[New Republic Fifth Fleet|New Republic Fifth Battle Group's]] [[21st Recon Group]] was dispatched in his [[T-65BR X-wing reconnaissance starfighter|Recon-X starfighter]] ''[[Jennie Lee]]'' to the N'zoth [[Star system/Legends|system]] to record statistical data on the Yevethan forces present. As the five-[[Standard minute/Legends|minute]] flight proceeded, the ''Jennie Lee''{{'s}} on-board [[R2-R-series astromech droid|R2-R unit]] was able to identify ''Aramadia''-class thrustships, ''Victory''-class and [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyers]], and a lone ''Executor''-class Star Dreadnought—the ''Pride of Yevetha''. Taggar's data, along with that of the other recon flights throughout the Cluster, was transmitted to the [[New Republic Defense Force headquarters]], where it was reviewed by Organa Solo, [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Gial Ackbar/Legends|Ackbar]], and others. The [[Tammarian]] [[Officer/Legends|officer]] [[Ayddar Nylykerka]] from the [[Asset Tracking]] office, who was on hand to identify the warships of the Black Fleet, was the one who confirmed the presence of the ''Intimidator'', knowing it only by its Imperial name. Nylykerka deduced the ship's identity from the additional shield generator tower on the warship's centerline—a trait common to all late-production ''Executor''-class ships. All present to hear the Tammarian's revelation realized that nothing in the [[New Republic Defense Fleet/Legends|New Republic Navy]] could outmatch a Super Star Destroyer.<ref name="SOL" />

In preparations for war with the Yevetha, the Chief of State dispatched her husband, [[Commodore/Legends|Commodore]] [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]], to assume command of the New Republic Fifth Fleet. En route, he was captured by the Yevetha<ref name="SOL" /> and [[Prison/Legends|imprisoned]] on N'zoth. He was later transferred to one of the ''Pride of Yevetha''{{'s}} unused cargo areas, where he and the other captives were intended to be used as living shields in the case of a New Republic attack. Unbeknownst to the Yevetha, members of the [[Fallanassi/Legends|Fallanassi]], a [[The Force/Legends|Force]]-based religion whose members were adept at creating [[Mind trick/Legends|illusions]], had managed to rescue most of the prisoners before their transfer to the Super Star Destroyer. A few Fallanassi had stayed behind in order to project the illusion that all the captives were still present.<ref name="TT" />

Spaar used the bridge of the ''Pride of Yevetha'' as the backdrop of a [[Hologram/Legends|holorecording]] designed to lead the Republic into believing that a "[[Grand Imperial Union]]" under a [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] "[[Tragg Brathis]]" had entered into a mutual agreement with the Duskhan League. Ackbar was able to confirm that both the Grand Imperial Union and Moff Brathis were non-existent, falsifications fabricated by Spaar, but the presence of a sizable amount of Imperial Star Destroyers in the recording was still enough to concern the Republic's leadership.<ref name="TT" />

[[File:NZoth.jpg|thumb|right|225px|N'zoth, over which the ''Intimidator'' was stationed for the majority of her operational history]]
Meanwhile, the [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]] and members of his family, including his son, [[Lumpawaroo/Legends|Lumpawarrump]], and cousins [[Jowdrrl]], [[Shoran]], and [[Dryanta]], launched a rescue mission to retrieve Solo from the ''Pride of Yevetha'' in the N'zoth system, using [[New Republic Fleet Intelligence]] information—such as a technical readout of a Super Star Destroyer—provided by the [[Yao]] [[Information broker/Legends|information broker]] [[Formayj]]. Piloting the [[YT-1300 light freighter/Legends|light freighter]] [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']], Chewbacca utilized [[Firecracker mine/Legends|firecracker mines]] to blind the Super Star Destroyer's [[Sensor/Legends|sensors]], allowing him to perform a quick [[Precision hyperspace jump|in-system hyperspace jump]] that brought him to 1,100 meters off the ''Pride of Yevetha''{{'s}} starboard bow without the gun batteries being able to track him. The Wookiees utilized a hull-cutting ring to board the warship, and Chewbacca, Shoran, and Lumpawarrump traversed through the Super Star Destroyer's converted detention blocks, severely damaging the breederies in the process. The strike team eventually arrived at the cargo hold where Solo was being kept. With Solo freed, the group escaped in the ''Millennium Falcon'', leaving many of the warship's Yevetha crew and soldiers dead from the raid.<ref name="TT" />

Following the attack, Spaar focused on replenishing the breederies, but this task was interrupted when three Republic [[stasis probe]]s—harbingers of an impending Republic attack—arrived in the N'zoth system. Spaar rushed to the bridge and ordered that the ''Pride of Yevetha'' break [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]] to intercept and destroy the probes, despite Proctor of Defense [[Tho Voota]]'s fears that N'zoth would be left undefended. As the ''Pride of Yevetha'' moved forward, the New Republic fleet emerged from [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]], just out of range of the Yevethan fleet's batteries. Republic [[General/Legends|General]] [[Etahn A'baht]] used the opportunity to deliver a final ultimatum to the Yevetha Viceroy.<ref name="TT" />

===The Empire strikes back===
{{Quote|We've been here too long already—nearly thirteen years on a nine-month detail. No, General. This is good-bye. We are taking back what is ours, starting with our freedom and these ships. We leave the Yevetha to you.|Major Sil Sorannan, to General Etahn A'baht|<ref name="TT" />}}
From the flagship's fire control center, the Imperial resistance leader, [[Major/Legends|Major]] [[Sil Sorannan]], listened patiently, marveling at how the New Republic had matured from the hit-and-run tactics of its predecessor, the Rebel Alliance. When the ''Pride of Yevetha'' was merely a minute away from being able to fire upon the nearest Republic vessel, Spaar issued his own challenge; simultaneously, Sorannan began his revolt. Using the slave circuitry that he and his Imperial allies had installed over the time of their captivity, Sorannan assumed command of the Black Fleet, bringing all the Star Destroyers to a halt. While the Yevethan ships continued their attack, Sorannan—identifying himself as acting [[Captain/Legends|captain]] of the ''Intimidator''—contacted A'baht and informed him that his Camp Pa'aal Squadron was leaving the battle. The long-delayed withdrawal of Black Sword Command was finally completed when the Star Destroyers made the jump to hyperspace,<ref name="TT" /> the Imperial throne world of [[Byss/Legends|Byss]], in the [[Deep Core/Legends|Deep Core]], as their destination.<ref name="NEC" />

With the heart of their fleet gone, the Yevetha were now outnumbered five-to-one. Spaar had been aboard the ''Intimidator'' when it departed, leaving the Yevetha leaderless. Despite these setbacks, the Duskhan League [[Battle of N'zoth|engaged]] the New Republic fleet with what they had left: [[D-type fighter]]s and forty-four ''Aramadia''-class thrustships. After inflicting heavy casualties, the Yevethan force was obliterated, and the Black Fleet Crisis came to an end. Spaar was taken captive by the Imperials aboard the ''Intimidator''; in revenge for the years of servitude, Sorannan had Spaar placed in an [[Escape pod/Legends|escape pod]] and launched into hyperspace, where he would eventually die and from which his remains would never be recovered.<ref name="TT" /> The Black Fleet eventually arrived in the [[Beshqek system]], but where Byss had once been was merely an [[Asteroid field/Legends|asteroid field]], created after the throne world had been destroyed by the [[Galaxy Gun]] [[Superweapon/Legends|superweapon]] approximately [[11 ABY/Legends|six years earlier]]. After a month, the fleet dispersed—some elements defected to the New Republic, while other warships joined up with some of the surviving factions of the Empire. The ''Intimidator'' vanished entirely; it was discovered [[21 ABY/Legends|four years later]] near the [[Unknown Regions/Legends|Unknown Regions]], abandoned and irreparably damaged.<ref name="NEC" />

==Commanders and crew==
Before the Yevethan revolt, the ''Intimidator'' was crewed by one-third of the amount of personnel required to operate the ship.<ref name="BTS" /> Approximately thirteen years later, in its service to the Duskhan League, the ''Pride of Yevetha''{{'s}} personnel included over 15,000 Yevetha and 513 veterans from Black Sword Command.<ref name="TT" />

===Jian Paret===
{{Main|Jian Paret}}
Jian Paret was the commander of the Imperial garrison on N'zoth in 4 ABY and the supervisor of Black Sword Command's withdrawal from the N'zoth system. He took the ''Intimidator'' as his command ship during the retreat, as it was the only spaceworthy vessel present at the Black-15 shipyard. Paret was captured during the Yevetha takeover of the Super Star Destroyer, and he watched helplessly as the Yevetha turned the warship's gun batteries on the evacuation shuttles. Immediately after, Nil Spaar executed him with a slash of the Yevetha's dew claw.<ref name="BTS" />

===Nil Spaar===
[[File:NilSpaarHS-NEGTC.png|thumb|right|200px|Nil Spaar used the ''Pride of Yevetha'' as his flagship]]
{{Main|Nil Spaar}}
Nil Spaar, before becoming viceroy of the Duskhan League, was a Yevetha resistance leader who personally led the uprising that captured the ''Intimidator''. By 16 ABY, he had ascended to the position of viceroy and was regarded as the ''darama'', or "chosen one." In the same year, he took the renamed ''Pride of Yevetha'' as his flagship, transferring most of the crew from his previous flagship, the thrustship ''Aramadia'', to the Super Star Destroyer, including his attaché Eri Palle, Proctor of Information Science Vor Duull, his breeding mates, and his honor guard. The Viceroy had three of his flagship's detention blocks converted into breederies for his children. Brutal and [[Speciesism|xenophobic]], Spaar oversaw the "cleansing" of the Koornacht Cluster and manipulated strife within the New Republic Senate to his advantage.<ref name="BTS" /> His plans came to an abrupt halt when the Imperials of Camp Pa'aal Squadron revolted and assumed control of the ''Intimidator'' and Black Fleet, taking Spaar captive in the process. Sil Sorannan put Spaar in an escape pod and launched him out into hyperspace, from which he was never recovered.<ref name="TT" />

===Dar Bille===
{{Main|Dar Bille}}
Dar Bille served as Nil Spaar's second-in-command during the Yevetha uprising, leading the second of two commando teams in the capture of the ''Intimidator'' during the failed Imperial withdrawal from N'zoth. Twelve years later, he was the primate, or commander, of the ''Pride of Yevetha'' during its operational service with the Duskhan League. Bille oversaw the ''Pride of Yevetha''{{'s}} participation in the Great Purge,<ref name="BTS" /> and he was still in command when the warship was seized by Camp Pa'aal Squadron during the Battle of N'zoth.<ref name="TT" />

===Sil Sorannan===
{{Main|Sil Sorannan}}
Sil Sorannan served as a major in Black Sword Command,<ref name="CSWE" /> having previously worked with a research team attempting to drop bombs from hyperspace and into realspace. During the Yevetha uprising, Sorannan was one of the 300,000 Imperials captured and forced to aid the Yevetha in operating their newly-acquired warships, including the ''Intimidator''. Over time, Sorannan became the leader of Camp Pa'aal Squadron, the Imperial resistance movement. He oversaw the construction and distribution of the slave circuit technology that, when the time was right, would give him complete control of the Black Fleet. While aboard the ''Pride of Yevetha''—which he often had to remind himself was properly known as the ''Intimidator''—he served in the fire control center of the Super Star Destroyer, maintaining the data server for target registry. Before the Battle of N'zoth could properly commence, Sorannan began his revolt and assumed command as acting captain of the ''Intimidator''. He activated the slave circuitry, tasking the Imperial warships with jumping into hyperspace<ref name="TT" /> and heading for the Imperial throne world Byss.<ref name="NEC" /> En route, he utilized his experience of dropping bombs into hyperspace when he left Nil Spaar stranded there in an escape pod.<ref name="TT" />

==Behind the scenes==
The ''Intimidator'' first appeared in the novel ''[[Before the Storm]]'', the first book of ''[[The Black Fleet Crisis]]'' trilogy, written by [[Michael P. Kube-McDowell]] and released in [[1996]]. The warship went on to appear in the other two books of the trilogy and was mentioned in later sourcebooks, which revealed its fate following the Battle of N'zoth. The ''Intimidator'' received its first visual depiction in ''[[The Official Star Wars Fact File 22]]'', released in [[May]] of [[2002]], six years after the publication of ''Before the Storm''.

Though the ''Intimidator'' is continually referred to as a "''Super''-class Star Destroyer" throughout the trilogy, the second book in the series, ''[[Shield of Lies]]'', was the first to provide the designation "''Executor''-class Star Destroyer" for the ''Intimidator'' and her sister ships. However, recent sources, such as the [[StarWars.com]] [[Databank (original)|Databank's]] [http://www.starwars.com/databank/starship/superstardestroyer/index.html Super Star Destroyer entry] and [http://www.wizards.com/default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20060309jc84 an article] on the [[Wizards of the Coast]] [[Wizards.com|Web site]], have updated the name as "''Executor''-class Star Dreadnought," in addition to increasing the length of the class from 8,000 meters (as reported in the books of the trilogy) to 19,000 meters. This article reflects these changes.

Although ''Before the Storm'' and ''Tyrant's Test'' make it clear that the majority of the ''Intimidator''{{'s}} Imperial crew was captured during the Yevetha revolt, both the ''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' and ''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Third Edition, Revised and Expanded]]'' state that the entire crew was killed during the takeover. This article assumes the [[canon]]icity of both ''Before the Storm'' and ''Tyrant's Test''.
